meta.title: aish — the AI-native shell
meta.description: aish is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers. v0.6 grows the toolbelt: aish pr, review, changelog, and ask join commit — PR descriptions, diff reviews, changelog entries, and one-shot questions from your terminal. Free software, MIT-licensed.

hero.badge: v0.6 shipped — pr, review, changelog & ask join the toolbelt

subhead.text: is an open-source, provider-agnostic AI shell for developers.
is built in — it turns your staged diff into a commit message you can accept, edit, or reject, with no install step beyond the binary itself.
— PR descriptions, severity-graded diff reviews, release notes, and one-shot questions piped straight from your terminal.

hero.installCaption: Linux & macOS · x86_64 / arm64 · or build from source with cargo.

features.heading: What aish is designed to be

items.title: Your git workflow, built in
items.body: aish commit turns your staged diff into a Conventional Commits message; aish pr drafts the PR title and body, aish review grades your diff by severity, and aish changelog writes release notes from your commits. Identical requests hit a deterministic local cache, every generation lands in an audit log, and aish usage turns that log into token and cost reports.

items.title: Provider-agnostic
items.body: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google Gemini, Ollama, and Kilo behind one config and model alias. Never locked to a single vendor — swap models with a flag.

items.title: Open & inspectable
items.body: MIT-licensed free software, shipped as a single static Rust binary. Read the specs, audit the design, send a patch — built in the open from day one.
